+ ---
+ 
+ ## Skipped Clarifications — Planning Decisions
+ 
+ The following questions from the &#96;/speckit.clarify&#96; session were deferred when
+ the user moved directly to &#96;/speckit.plan&#96;. Answers are encoded here as binding
+ planning decisions and propagated into the implementation contracts.
+ 
+ | Deferred Question | Planning Decision | Rationale |
+ |-------------------|-------------------|-----------|
+ | Q2 — Retry budget for OptimisticConcurrencyError | **Max 3 retries, exponential backoff (100 ms → 200 ms → 400 ms) at the MCP tool / command handler layer; event store surfaces error immediately on every collision** | Challenge DESIGN.md explicitly asks for retry strategy and error rate estimate; 3 retries matches Marten's default retry budget; exponential backoff reduces thundering herd under peak load (100 apps × 4 agents) |
+ | Q3 — Outbox destination scope | **Internal-only this week: &#96;destination&#96; field records intent (e.g., &#96;"projection-daemon"&#96;, &#96;"kafka-week-10"&#96;); no external bus consumer is implemented; outbox table is written atomically but not polled** | Single-database architecture; Kafka/Redis integration deferred to Week 10 per challenge stack table |
+ | Q4 — Daemon default retry count and lag SLO breach policy | **Default 3 retries per event per projection; if any projection exceeds its SLO lag by 3× (ApplicationSummary &gt; 1,500 ms, ComplianceAuditView &gt; 6,000 ms) for 5 consecutive poll cycles, daemon logs CRITICAL and continues; no automatic restart** | Fault-tolerant daemon must not crash or self-restart unpredictably; CRITICAL log is the alerting signal for operators |
+ | Q5 — ComplianceAuditView snapshot trigger strategy | **Event-count trigger: snapshot taken every 100 events per application in the compliance stream; stored as JSONB in a &#96;compliance_snapshots&#96; table alongside position; temporal query scans from the nearest preceding snapshot** | Event-count is deterministic and proportional to data growth; avoids snapshot storm on time-based triggers under variable write load; aligns with Marten's default snapshot strategy |
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-001 — Async PostgreSQL Driver: asyncpg vs psycopg3
+ 
+ **Decision**: &#96;asyncpg&#96; as primary async driver.
+ 
+ **Rationale**:
+ - asyncpg has the highest raw throughput for bulk event appends; benchmarks show
+   2–3× higher write throughput than psycopg3 for the append-heavy event store workload
+ - asyncpg uses a binary wire protocol, reducing serialisation overhead on JSONB columns
+ - asyncpg's &#96;Connection.transaction()&#96; context manager is idiomatic for the
+   atomic &#96;append + outbox&#96; operation required by FR-010
+ - psycopg3 async is an acceptable fallback; the interface is identical for this use case
+ 
+ **Implementation note**: All database interactions MUST use &#96;asyncpg.Pool&#96; for
+ connection pooling. Never use &#96;asyncio.create_task&#96; with raw connections — always
+ acquire from pool.
+ 
+ **Alternatives considered**:
+ - &#96;psycopg3&#96; async: compatible; slightly lower write throughput; acceptable fallback
+ - &#96;SQLAlchemy async&#96;: rejected — ORM abstraction over event tables is forbidden by
+   Principle VII of the constitution
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-002 — Optimistic Concurrency Implementation in PostgreSQL
+ 
+ **Decision**: Advisory-lock-free approach using the &#96;UNIQUE (stream_id, stream_position)&#96; constraint combined with a stream version check inside a single transaction.
+ 
+ **Rationale**:
+ The challenge schema includes &#96;CONSTRAINT uq_stream_position UNIQUE (stream_id, stream_position)&#96;.
+ The correct concurrency implementation:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ -- Step 1: Fetch current version (inside transaction)
+ SELECT current_version FROM event_streams
+ WHERE stream_id = $1
+ FOR UPDATE;                          -- row-level lock on this stream only
+ 
+ -- Step 2: Check expected version
+ -- If current_version != expected_version → raise OptimisticConcurrencyError
+ 
+ -- Step 3: Insert events (positions = current_version + 1, +2, ...)
+ INSERT INTO events (stream_id, stream_position, ...)
+ VALUES ($1, $current_version + 1, ...);
+ 
+ -- Step 4: Update stream version
+ UPDATE event_streams SET current_version = $new_version WHERE stream_id = $1;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ The &#96;SELECT ... FOR UPDATE&#96; on the &#96;event_streams&#96; row serialises concurrent
+ appends to the same stream without locking the entire &#96;events&#96; table. Two agents
+ appending to different streams do not block each other.
+ 
+ **Why NOT advisory locks**: Advisory locks require separate lock acquisition,
+ are not automatically released on connection pool recycle, and add complexity
+ without benefit given the &#96;FOR UPDATE&#96; approach is sufficient.
+ 
+ **Why NOT application-layer optimistic locking without DB lock**: Pure
+ compare-and-set at the application layer would require a retry loop within the
+ transaction, reintroducing the race condition on the &#96;UPDATE&#96;.
+ 
+ **OptimisticConcurrencyError trigger**: If &#96;current_version != expected_version&#96;
+ after the &#96;SELECT FOR UPDATE&#96;, the transaction is rolled back and
+ &#96;OptimisticConcurrencyError&#96; is raised immediately with:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ OptimisticConcurrencyError(
+     stream_id=stream_id,
+     expected_version=expected_version,
+     actual_version=current_version,
+ )
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Retry strategy** (MCP tool layer, not event store layer):
+ - Maximum 3 retries
+ - Backoff: 100 ms, 200 ms, 400 ms (exponential)
+ - On 4th failure: raise &#96;ConcurrencyExhaustedError&#96; with full context
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-003 — CQRS Projection Pattern: asyncio Background Task
+ 
+ **Decision**: &#96;ProjectionDaemon&#96; runs as a persistent &#96;asyncio.Task&#96; inside the
+ same process as the MCP server, polling &#96;events&#96; table by &#96;global_position&#96;.
+ 
+ **Rationale**:
+ - Avoids inter-process coordination (no Redis, no Celery) for the single-database
+   architecture mandated by the challenge
+ - &#96;asyncio.Task&#96; lifecycle aligns naturally with the MCP server's lifespan
+ - Checkpoint management via &#96;projection_checkpoints&#96; table is atomic within
+   the same PostgreSQL connection pool
+ - The challenge's &#96;ProjectionDaemon&#96; interface (&#96;run_forever&#96;, &#96;_process_batch&#96;)
+   maps directly to a background asyncio loop
+ 
+ **Daemon lifecycle**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ daemon_task = asyncio.create_task(daemon.run_forever(poll_interval_ms=100))
+ # Started on MCP server startup; cancelled on shutdown
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Fault tolerance design**:
+ 1. Each projection handler is called inside &#96;try/except Exception&#96;
+ 2. On handler failure: increment retry counter for that event+projection pair
+ 3. After 3 failures: log error with event_id + projection_name, record in a
+    &#96;projection_errors&#96; table (see data-model.md), skip event for this projection
+ 4. Daemon continues to next event — it NEVER raises to the outer loop
+ 5. &#96;projection_errors&#96; table enables post-mortem and manual replay
+ 
+ **Checkpoint update**: Updated after each successful batch (not per-event)
+ to reduce write amplification. Batch size configurable (default 500 events).
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-004 — UpcasterRegistry Pattern
+ 
+ **Decision**: Decorator-based registry with automatic chain traversal on event load.
+ 
+ **Rationale**:
+ The challenge provides the exact &#96;UpcasterRegistry&#96; class signature with
+ &#96;@registry.register(event_type, from_version)&#96; decorator pattern. This maps
+ directly to a dict-based registry keyed on &#96;(event_type, version)&#96; tuples.
+ 
+ **Key implementation notes**:
+ 1. The registry MUST be a singleton (module-level instance) to ensure all
+    upcasters registered with &#96;@registry.register(...)&#96; in &#96;upcasters.py&#96; are
+    available when &#96;EventStore.load_stream()&#96; calls &#96;registry.upcast(event)&#96;
+ 2. &#96;StoredEvent.with_payload(new_payload, version=v+1)&#96; must return a new
+    &#96;StoredEvent&#96; instance (immutable pattern) — never mutate the original
+ 3. &#96;load_stream()&#96; MUST call &#96;registry.upcast(event)&#96; for every loaded event
+    before returning it to callers — the upcasting must be transparent
+ 4. The raw DB payload MUST be read but never written by the upcast path —
+    immutability test verifies this
+ 
+ **CreditAnalysisCompleted v1→v2 inference strategy**:
+ - &#96;model_version&#96;: infer from &#96;recorded_at&#96; timestamp using a known deployment
+   timeline lookup table (e.g., events before 2026-01-01 → &#96;"legacy-pre-2026"&#96;)
+ - &#96;confidence_score&#96;: &#96;None&#96; — genuinely unknown; fabrication would create false
+   precision in regulatory audit records, making the null-vs-fabrication choice
+   a compliance decision, not a convenience decision
+ - &#96;regulatory_basis&#96;: infer from the regulation_set_version active at &#96;recorded_at&#96;
+ 
+ **DecisionGenerated v1→v2 inference strategy**:
+ - &#96;model_versions{}&#96;: reconstruct by loading each session's &#96;AgentContextLoaded&#96;
+   event from the &#96;AgentSession&#96; stream; this requires a store lookup inside the
+   upcaster — document the O(n) performance implication in DESIGN.md where
+   n = number of contributing agent sessions (typically 3–4, bounded)
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-005 — MCP Python SDK: FastMCP vs Low-Level
+ 
+ **Decision**: FastMCP (decorator-based API from the &#96;mcp&#96; Python SDK).
+ 
+ **Rationale**:
+ - FastMCP's &#96;@mcp.tool()&#96; and &#96;@mcp.resource()&#96; decorators map directly to the
+   challenge's "8 tools + 6 resources" structure
+ - FastMCP handles protocol-level serialisation; implementation focuses on
+   domain logic
+ - Pydantic models integrate natively for tool input validation (matching FR-009's
+   "Schema validation via Pydantic" requirement for &#96;submit_application&#96;)
+ - Error responses: FastMCP supports raising exceptions that serialise to structured
+   MCP error responses — use custom exception types (&#96;OptimisticConcurrencyError&#96;,
+   &#96;DomainError&#96;, &#96;PreconditionFailed&#96;) that serialise to typed error objects
+ 
+ **Tool error contract**:
+ All tool errors MUST return a structured JSON object:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "error_type": "OptimisticConcurrencyError",
+   "message": "Stream version mismatch",
+   "stream_id": "loan-app-123",
+   "expected_version": 3,
+   "actual_version": 5,
+   "suggested_action": "reload_stream_and_retry"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ This is not just a string — it is a typed object the consuming LLM can reason about.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-006 — Cryptographic Hash Chain (SHA-256)
+ 
+ **Decision**: &#96;hashlib.sha256&#96; (stdlib) with canonical JSON serialisation for
+ deterministic hashing.
+ 
+ **Rationale**:
+ - No external crypto library needed; stdlib &#96;hashlib&#96; is sufficient for SHA-256
+ - Canonical serialisation: &#96;json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)&#96;
+   produces deterministic output across Python versions
+ - Chain construction: &#96;new_hash = sha256(previous_hash.encode() + payload_hash)&#96;
+   where each event's contribution is &#96;sha256(json.dumps(event.payload, sort_keys=True))&#96;
+ 
+ **Hash chain integrity algorithm**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ previous_hash = "0" * 64  (genesis hash for first check)
+ for event in events_since_last_check:
+     event_hash = sha256(canonical_json(event.payload))
+     chain_hash = sha256(previous_hash + event_hash)
+     previous_hash = chain_hash
+ new_hash = chain_hash
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Tamper detection**: If &#96;sha256(previous_hash + recalculated_event_hashes) !=
+ stored_integrity_hash&#96;, then &#96;tamper_detected = True&#96;, &#96;chain_valid = False&#96;.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-007 — Enterprise Stack Translation
+ 
+ *This section fulfils the user requirement for an "Enterprise Stack Translation"
+ section mapping the PostgreSQL implementation to Marten/Wolverine (.NET) and
+ EventStoreDB. This content appears in expanded form in DESIGN.md Section 5.*
+ 
+ ### PostgreSQL Implementation → EventStoreDB 24.x
+ 
+ | This Implementation | EventStoreDB Equivalent | What EventStoreDB Gives You for Free |
+ |---------------------|------------------------|--------------------------------------|
+ | &#96;events&#96; table with &#96;stream_id + stream_position&#96; | Native stream storage; each stream is a first-class object | No schema management; built-in stream compaction and truncation |
+ | &#96;event_streams.current_version&#96; + &#96;SELECT FOR UPDATE&#96; | &#96;expectedRevision&#96; parameter on &#96;AppendToStreamAsync&#96; | Native OCC at the protocol level; no application-layer lock needed |
+ | &#96;load_stream(stream_id)&#96; via SQL &#96;WHERE stream_id = $1&#96; | &#96;ReadStreamAsync(streamName, StreamPosition.Start)&#96; | Optimised stream reads; forward and backward cursor support |
+ | &#96;load_all(from_global_position)&#96; async generator | &#96;$all&#96; stream subscription with &#96;FromAll(Position.Start)&#96; | Built-in global ordering; persistent subscriptions survive process restart |
+ | &#96;ProjectionDaemon&#96; polling &#96;global_position&#96; | EventStoreDB persistent subscriptions with consumer group checkpointing | Distributed checkpoint management; competing consumers across nodes |
+ | &#96;projection_checkpoints&#96; table | Persistent subscription checkpoint stored in EventStoreDB itself | No external checkpoint storage; auto-resume on reconnect |
+ | &#96;outbox&#96; table + manual publisher | EventStoreDB's built-in persistent subscriptions act as the outbox | No separate outbox needed; at-least-once delivery is native |
+ | &#96;archive_stream&#96; setting &#96;archived_at&#96; | Stream soft-deletion / truncation via &#96;$tb&#96; (truncate before) metadata | Native stream lifecycle management |
+ 
+ **What EventStoreDB gives you that this implementation works harder to achieve**:
+ 1. **Persistent subscriptions**: EventStoreDB's competing consumer groups handle
+    multi-node projection distribution natively; this implementation's
+    &#96;ProjectionDaemon&#96; requires a coordination primitive (e.g., Redis SETNX or
+    PostgreSQL advisory lock) to achieve the same on multiple nodes.
+ 2. **Built-in $all stream**: EventStoreDB's global stream is a native construct;
+    this implementation's &#96;global_position BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY&#96;
+    achieves the same ordering but requires careful handling of identity gaps under
+    transaction rollbacks.
+ 3. **Native gRPC streaming**: EventStoreDB streams events to subscribers via gRPC
+    without polling; this implementation polls at 100 ms intervals, adding up to
+    100 ms latency to the projection pipeline.
+ 
+ ### PostgreSQL Implementation → Marten 7.x + Wolverine
+ 
+ | This Implementation | Marten/Wolverine Equivalent | Notes |
+ |---------------------|-----------------------------|-------|
+ | &#96;EventStore.append()&#96; with OCC | &#96;IDocumentSession.Events.Append(streamKey, expectedVersion, events)&#96; followed by &#96;SaveChangesAsync()&#96; | Marten handles the &#96;mt_events&#96; table schema; same OCC semantics |
+ | &#96;LoanApplicationAggregate.load()&#96; + &#96;_apply()&#96; | &#96;IQuerySession.Events.AggregateStreamAsync&lt;LoanApplication&gt;(streamId)&#96; | Marten calls the &#96;Apply(event)&#96; methods via reflection; same pattern |
+ | &#96;UpcasterRegistry&#96; + &#96;@registry.register&#96; | &#96;IEventTransformation&#96; implementations registered in &#96;StoreOptions&#96; | Marten's upcasting uses the same &#96;IEventTransformation&#96; → &#96;from_version → to_version&#96; chain |
+ | &#96;ProjectionDaemon.run_forever()&#96; | Marten's Async Daemon (&#96;IDocumentStore.BuildProjectionDaemon()&#96;) | Marten's daemon supports distributed execution across multiple nodes via leader election |
+ | &#96;ApplicationSummary&#96; projection | &#96;IProjection&#96; or &#96;MultiStreamProjection&lt;ApplicationSummary&gt;&#96; | Marten handles SQL upserts; developer writes only &#96;Apply(event)&#96; methods |
+ | &#96;ComplianceAuditView.get_compliance_at(timestamp)&#96; | &#96;IQuerySession.Events.AggregateStreamAsync&lt;ComplianceRecord&gt;(id, timestamp: asOf)&#96; | Marten supports native temporal queries via &#96;asOf&#96; parameter |
+ | &#96;generate_regulatory_package()&#96; | Custom — Marten has no native regulatory package concept | Marten provides the event query primitives; package assembly is application code in both cases |
+ | &#96;run_integrity_check()&#96; + SHA-256 chain | Custom — no native hash chain in Marten | Marten provides no cryptographic audit chain; this is a domain feature in both stacks |
+ 
+ **Advising a .NET client**:
+ When a client already has a Marten/Wolverine stack, the business logic (aggregates,
+ business rules, upcasters) ports directly: the &#96;_apply&#96; pattern becomes &#96;Apply&#96;
+ methods, the &#96;UpcasterRegistry&#96; becomes &#96;IEventTransformation&#96; registrations, and
+ command handlers become Wolverine message handlers. The event schema (stream IDs,
+ payload shapes, version numbers) is identical. The migration effort is infrastructure
+ rewiring, not domain rewrite.
+ 
+ **Advising a client considering EventStoreDB**:
+ EventStoreDB eliminates the need for: (a) the &#96;event_streams&#96; table, (b) the
+ &#96;global_position&#96; identity column, (c) the &#96;ProjectionDaemon&#96; polling loop, and
+ (d) the &#96;outbox&#96; table. The trade-off is operational complexity of a dedicated
+ event store service vs. the "Postgres is everywhere" deployment simplicity.
+ For teams already running PostgreSQL, the custom implementation in this challenge
+ is the right first deployment; EventStoreDB becomes the migration target when
+ throughput exceeds ~10,000 events/second sustained.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-008 — Snapshot Strategy for ComplianceAuditView Temporal Queries
+ 
+ **Decision**: Event-count trigger — snapshot taken every 100 compliance events
+ per application; stored in &#96;compliance_snapshots&#96; table.
+ 
+ **Implementation**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE compliance_snapshots (
+   application_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
+   snapshot_position BIGINT NOT NULL,   -- global_position of last event included
+   snapshot_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
+   state_json        JSONB NOT NULL,
+   PRIMARY KEY (application_id, snapshot_position)
+ );
+ CREATE INDEX idx_compliance_snapshots_app ON compliance_snapshots
+   (application_id, snapshot_position DESC);
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Temporal query algorithm** (&#96;get_compliance_at(application_id, timestamp)&#96;):
+ 1. Find nearest preceding snapshot: &#96;SELECT * FROM compliance_snapshots WHERE
+    application_id = $1 AND snapshot_at &lt;= $2 ORDER BY snapshot_at DESC LIMIT 1&#96;
+ 2. If no snapshot: replay from position 0
+ 3. Load compliance events from &#96;snapshot_position&#96; to events where
+    &#96;recorded_at &lt;= $2&#96;
+ 4. Apply events to snapshot state; return result
+ 
+ **Snapshot invalidation**: Snapshots are never invalidated — they represent
+ historical truth. On &#96;rebuild_from_scratch()&#96;, the &#96;compliance_snapshots&#96; table
+ is also truncated and rebuilt. Snapshots for an application are append-only;
+ they are never updated or deleted outside of a full rebuild.
+ 
+ **Why 100 events**: The average Apex application has ~15–20 compliance events
+ (checks × rules). 100 events ≈ 5–7 applications' worth of compliance data
+ in the stream, making snapshots rare enough to not cause storage pressure
+ while keeping &#96;get_compliance_at&#96; replay cost bounded.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-009 — Gas Town Agent Memory Reconstruction
+ 
+ **Decision**: Token-budget-aware summarisation with verbatim preservation of
+ the last 3 events and any PENDING/ERROR state events.
+ 
+ **Reconstruction algorithm**:
+ 1. Load all events from &#96;AgentSession&#96; stream for &#96;agent_id + session_id&#96;
+ 2. Walk events in order; separate into: "old context" (all except last 3 +
+    PENDING/ERROR) vs "preserve verbatim" (last 3 + PENDING/ERROR)
+ 3. For "old context" events: generate one prose sentence per event type
+    (e.g., "Agent loaded context from stream position 0 at 14:32 UTC")
+ 4. Count tokens in verbatim section; subtract from &#96;token_budget&#96;
+ 5. Fill remaining budget with prose summary of old context (truncate oldest first)
+ 6. If last event has no corresponding completion event: set
+    &#96;session_health_status = "NEEDS_RECONCILIATION"&#96;; include explicit
+    "INCOMPLETE ACTION" marker at the end of context_text
+ 
+ **NEEDS_RECONCILIATION detection**: An event is "partial" if it belongs to a
+ class requiring a paired completion (e.g., &#96;CreditAnalysisRequested&#96; without
+ a subsequent &#96;CreditAnalysisCompleted&#96; in the same session stream for the same
+ &#96;application_id&#96;).
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## R-010 — DOMAIN_NOTES.md Pre-Implementation Content Outline
+ 
+ *The following question answers are research-level findings that the
+ &#96;DOMAIN_NOTES.md&#96; document must develop in full before any implementation
+ begins (Principle VI, Principle VIII Phase Gate 0).*
+ 
+ **Q1 — EDA vs ES distinction**:
+ A LangChain callback trace is EDA — events are fired for observability and can
+ be dropped; they are not the source of truth. Redesigning with The Ledger:
+ replace callbacks with &#96;EventStore.append()&#96; calls; the agent's context is
+ reconstructed from the stream on restart; the trace IS the state.
+ 
+ **Q2 — Rejected aggregate boundary**:
+ Alternative considered: merging &#96;ComplianceRecord&#96; into &#96;LoanApplication&#96;.
+ Rejected because: a compliance check and a loan state transition can occur
+ concurrently (two compliance agents checking different rules in parallel).
+ Merged aggregate would require a single lock on the loan stream for all compliance
+ work, serialising what should be parallel writes. Separate aggregate allows
+ N compliance agents to write to &#96;compliance-{id}&#96; while the loan state machine
+ progresses independently.
+ 
+ **Q3 — Concurrency trace**:
+ Both agents acquire: &#96;SELECT current_version FROM event_streams WHERE stream_id =
+ 'loan-app-123' FOR UPDATE&#96;. One wins the row lock. It reads version=3, checks
+ expected_version=3 ✓, inserts event at stream_position=4, updates
+ current_version=4, commits. The second agent then acquires the lock, reads
+ version=4, checks expected_version=3 ✗, rolls back, raises
+ &#96;OptimisticConcurrencyError(expected=3, actual=4)&#96;.
+ 
+ **Q4 — Projection lag consequence**:
+ The system serves the stale read from the &#96;ApplicationSummary&#96; projection. The
+ UI receives the old credit limit value. Mitigation: the &#96;ledger://ledger/health&#96;
+ response includes current projection lag; the UI can display a staleness indicator
+ ("Data as of N ms ago") when lag exceeds a configurable threshold (e.g., 1,000 ms).
+ The UI should never block on projection consistency — it accepts eventual
+ consistency and communicates it.
+ 
+ **Q5 — CreditAnalysisCompleted v1→v2 upcaster**:
+ See R-004 above. Inference: &#96;model_version&#96; from timestamp lookup; &#96;confidence_score&#96;
+ = null (regulatory compliance reason documented); &#96;regulatory_basis&#96; from
+ regulation version active at &#96;recorded_at&#96;.
+ 
+ **Q6 — Marten Async Daemon parallel**:
+ Coordination primitive: PostgreSQL advisory lock (&#96;pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtext('projection-daemon-leader'))&#96;)
+ for leader election across multiple nodes. The failure mode it guards against:
+ split-brain projection processing where two daemon nodes process the same
+ global_position range and apply duplicate updates to projection tables. Only
+ the lock holder processes events; others stand by and poll the lock periodically.
+ 
```

@@ -1,1 +1,629 @@
- 
+ # Data Model: The Ledger — Agentic Event Store
+ 
+ **Branch**: &#96;001-ledger-event-store&#96; | **Date**: 2026-03-16
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 1. PostgreSQL Schema Tables
+ 
+ ### 1.1 &#96;events&#96; — The Core Store
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE events (
+   event_id         UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
+   stream_id        TEXT NOT NULL,
+   stream_position  BIGINT NOT NULL,            -- position within stream (1-based)
+   global_position  BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,  -- global ordering across all streams
+   event_type       TEXT NOT NULL,              -- e.g. "ApplicationSubmitted"
+   event_version    SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
+   payload          JSONB NOT NULL,             -- event-type-specific data
+   metadata         JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,  -- correlation_id, causation_id, etc.
+   recorded_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT clock_timestamp(),
+   CONSTRAINT uq_stream_position UNIQUE (stream_id, stream_position)
+ );
+ 
+ CREATE INDEX idx_events_stream_id ON events (stream_id, stream_position);
+ CREATE INDEX idx_events_global_pos ON events (global_position);
+ CREATE INDEX idx_events_type ON events (event_type);
+ CREATE INDEX idx_events_recorded ON events (recorded_at);
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Column justifications** (required in DESIGN.md):
+ - &#96;event_id&#96;: UUID primary key enables direct event lookup without stream context
+ - &#96;stream_id&#96;: text (not FK) for schema flexibility; format encodes aggregate type
+ - &#96;stream_position&#96;: optimistic concurrency lock key; enforced by &#96;uq_stream_position&#96;
+ - &#96;global_position&#96;: identity column provides total ordering for projection replay
+ - &#96;event_type&#96;: enables &#96;load_all(event_types=[...])&#96; filtering without full scan
+ - &#96;event_version&#96;: enables &#96;UpcasterRegistry&#96; chain lookup
+ - &#96;payload&#96;: JSONB for indexed querying of event data (e.g., &#96;application_id&#96; inside payload)
+ - &#96;metadata&#96;: correlation_id, causation_id, source_system — not domain data
+ - &#96;recorded_at&#96;: &#96;clock_timestamp()&#96; (not &#96;now()&#96;) captures wall time, not transaction time
+ 
+ ### 1.2 &#96;event_streams&#96; — Stream Registry
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE event_streams (
+   stream_id        TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+   aggregate_type   TEXT NOT NULL,              -- e.g. "LoanApplication"
+   current_version  BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- optimistic concurrency version
+   created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
+   archived_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,                -- NULL = active; set on archive_stream()
+   metadata         JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **&#96;aggregate_type&#96; values**: &#96;LoanApplication&#96;, &#96;AgentSession&#96;, &#96;ComplianceRecord&#96;, &#96;AuditLedger&#96;
+ 
+ ### 1.3 &#96;projection_checkpoints&#96; — Daemon State
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE projection_checkpoints (
+   projection_name  TEXT PRIMARY KEY,           -- e.g. "application_summary"
+   last_position    BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- last global_position processed
+   updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 1.4 &#96;outbox&#96; — Guaranteed Delivery
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE outbox (
+   id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
+   event_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES events(event_id),
+   destination      TEXT NOT NULL,              -- routing intent; e.g. "kafka-week-10"
+   payload          JSONB NOT NULL,
+   created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
+   published_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,                -- NULL = unpublished
+   attempts         SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Scope in this implementation**: outbox rows are written atomically with events
+ (FR-010); no outbox publisher process is implemented this week — that is Week 10.
+ 
+ ### 1.5 &#96;compliance_snapshots&#96; — Temporal Query Support
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE compliance_snapshots (
+   application_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
+   snapshot_position BIGINT NOT NULL,           -- global_position of last included event
+   snapshot_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
+   state_json        JSONB NOT NULL,            -- serialised ComplianceAuditView state
+   PRIMARY KEY (application_id, snapshot_position)
+ );
+ CREATE INDEX idx_compliance_snapshots_app
+   ON compliance_snapshots (application_id, snapshot_position DESC);
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Trigger**: taken every 100 compliance events per application (see research.md R-008).
+ 
+ ### 1.6 &#96;projection_errors&#96; — Daemon Fault Record
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE projection_errors (
+   id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
+   projection_name  TEXT NOT NULL,
+   event_id         UUID NOT NULL,
+   global_position  BIGINT NOT NULL,
+   error_message    TEXT NOT NULL,
+   attempts         SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
+   recorded_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 2. Pydantic Models
+ 
+ ### 2.1 Base Types
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ # src/models/events.py
+ 
+ class BaseEvent(BaseModel):
+     """All domain events inherit from this. event_type auto-set to class name."""
+     model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)       # immutable after construction
+     event_type: ClassVar[str]                    # set by each subclass
+     event_version: ClassVar[int] = 1
+ 
+ class StoredEvent(BaseModel):
+     """Wrapper around a persisted event, including store metadata."""
+     model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)
+     event_id: UUID
+     stream_id: str
+     stream_position: int
+     global_position: int
+     event_type: str
+     event_version: int
+     payload: dict[str, Any]
+     metadata: dict[str, Any]
+     recorded_at: datetime
+ 
+     def with_payload(self, new_payload: dict, version: int) -&gt; "StoredEvent":
+         """Return new StoredEvent with updated payload/version; original unchanged."""
+         return self.model_copy(update={"payload": new_payload, "event_version": version})
+ 
+ class StreamMetadata(BaseModel):
+     stream_id: str
+     aggregate_type: str
+     current_version: int
+     created_at: datetime
+     archived_at: datetime | None
+     metadata: dict[str, Any]
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 2.2 Custom Exceptions
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class OptimisticConcurrencyError(Exception):
+     def __init__(self, stream_id: str, expected_version: int, actual_version: int):
+         self.stream_id = stream_id
+         self.expected_version = expected_version
+         self.actual_version = actual_version
+         self.suggested_action = "reload_stream_and_retry"
+ 
+ class ConcurrencyExhaustedError(Exception):
+     """Raised after max_retries OptimisticConcurrencyErrors on the same operation."""
+     def __init__(self, stream_id: str, attempts: int):
+         self.stream_id = stream_id
+         self.attempts = attempts
+ 
+ class DomainError(Exception):
+     """Raised by aggregates when a business rule is violated."""
+     def __init__(self, rule: str, message: str):
+         self.rule = rule
+         self.message = message
+ 
+ class StreamNotFoundError(Exception):
+     def __init__(self, stream_id: str):
+         self.stream_id = stream_id
+ 
+ class PreconditionFailed(Exception):
+     """Raised by MCP tools when a precondition (e.g., active session) is unmet."""
+     def __init__(self, precondition: str, suggested_action: str):
+         self.precondition = precondition
+         self.suggested_action = suggested_action
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+

+ 
+ ## 4. Aggregate State Types
+ 
+ ### 4.1 &#96;LoanApplicationAggregate&#96;
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class ApplicationState(str, Enum):
+     SUBMITTED               = "Submitted"
+     AWAITING_ANALYSIS       = "AwaitingAnalysis"
+     ANALYSIS_COMPLETE       = "AnalysisComplete"
+     COMPLIANCE_REVIEW       = "ComplianceReview"
+     PENDING_DECISION        = "PendingDecision"
+     APPROVED_PENDING_HUMAN  = "ApprovedPendingHuman"
+     DECLINED_PENDING_HUMAN  = "DeclinedPendingHuman"
+     FINAL_APPROVED          = "FinalApproved"
+     FINAL_DECLINED          = "FinalDeclined"
+ 
+ # Valid state machine transitions (enforced in _apply):
+ VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[ApplicationState, set[ApplicationState]] = {
+     ApplicationState.SUBMITTED:              {ApplicationState.AWAITING_ANALYSIS},
+     ApplicationState.AWAITING_ANALYSIS:      {ApplicationState.ANALYSIS_COMPLETE},
+     ApplicationState.ANALYSIS_COMPLETE:      {ApplicationState.COMPLIANCE_REVIEW},
+     ApplicationState.COMPLIANCE_REVIEW:      {ApplicationState.PENDING_DECISION},
+     ApplicationState.PENDING_DECISION:       {ApplicationState.APPROVED_PENDING_HUMAN,
+                                               ApplicationState.DECLINED_PENDING_HUMAN},
+     ApplicationState.APPROVED_PENDING_HUMAN: {ApplicationState.FINAL_APPROVED,
+                                               ApplicationState.FINAL_DECLINED},
+     ApplicationState.DECLINED_PENDING_HUMAN: {ApplicationState.FINAL_APPROVED,
+                                               ApplicationState.FINAL_DECLINED},
+ }
+ 
+ class LoanApplicationAggregate:
+     application_id: str
+     state: ApplicationState
+     version: int                      # = last stream_position
+     applicant_id: str | None
+     requested_amount: Decimal | None
+     approved_amount: Decimal | None
+     risk_tier: str | None
+     confidence_score: float | None
+     compliance_checks_required: list[str]
+     compliance_checks_passed: list[str]
+     credit_analysis_locked: bool      # True after first CreditAnalysisCompleted
+     contributing_sessions: list[str]  # for causal chain validation
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 4.2 &#96;AgentSessionAggregate&#96;
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class SessionHealthStatus(str, Enum):
+     ACTIVE               = "ACTIVE"
+     NEEDS_RECONCILIATION = "NEEDS_RECONCILIATION"
+     COMPLETED            = "COMPLETED"
+ 
+ class AgentSessionAggregate:
+     agent_id: str
+     session_id: str
+     version: int
+     context_loaded: bool              # True after AgentContextLoaded event
+     model_version: str | None
+     completed_application_ids: set[str]
+     health_status: SessionHealthStatus
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 4.3 &#96;ComplianceRecordAggregate&#96;
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class ComplianceRecordAggregate:
+     application_id: str
+     version: int
+     regulation_set_version: str | None
+     checks_required: list[str]
+     checks_passed: list[str]
+     checks_failed: list[str]          # with failure reasons
+     clearance_granted: bool
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 4.4 &#96;AuditLedgerAggregate&#96;
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class AuditLedgerAggregate:
+     entity_type: str
+     entity_id: str
+     version: int
+     last_integrity_hash: str          # "0"*64 for genesis
+     last_check_at: datetime | None
+     last_check_rate_key: str          # for rate limiting: "audit-{entity_type}-{entity_id}"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 5. Projection Schemas
+ 
+ ### 5.1 &#96;ApplicationSummary&#96; Table
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE application_summary (
+   application_id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+   state                   TEXT NOT NULL,
+   applicant_id            TEXT,
+   requested_amount_usd    NUMERIC(15,2),
+   approved_amount_usd     NUMERIC(15,2),
+   risk_tier               TEXT,
+   fraud_score             NUMERIC(5,4),
+   compliance_status       TEXT,        -- "PENDING" | "PASSED" | "FAILED"
+   decision                TEXT,        -- "APPROVE" | "DECLINE" | "REFER" | NULL
+   agent_sessions_completed TEXT[],     -- array of session stream IDs
+   last_event_type         TEXT,
+   last_event_at           TIMESTAMPTZ,
+   human_reviewer_id       TEXT,
+   final_decision_at       TIMESTAMPTZ
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 5.2 &#96;AgentPerformanceLedger&#96; Table
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE agent_performance_ledger (
+   agent_id                TEXT NOT NULL,
+   model_version           TEXT NOT NULL,
+   analyses_completed      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
+   decisions_generated     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
+   avg_confidence_score    NUMERIC(5,4),
+   avg_duration_ms         NUMERIC(10,2),
+   approve_rate            NUMERIC(5,4),
+   decline_rate            NUMERIC(5,4),
+   refer_rate              NUMERIC(5,4),
+   human_override_rate     NUMERIC(5,4),
+   first_seen_at           TIMESTAMPTZ,
+   last_seen_at            TIMESTAMPTZ,
+   PRIMARY KEY (agent_id, model_version)
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 5.3 &#96;ComplianceAuditView&#96; Table
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;sql
+ CREATE TABLE compliance_audit_view (
+   application_id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+   regulation_set_version  TEXT,
+   checks_required         TEXT[],
+   checks_passed           JSONB,   -- [{rule_id, rule_version, evaluation_timestamp, evidence_hash}]
+   checks_failed           JSONB,   -- [{rule_id, rule_version, failure_reason, remediation_required}]
+   clearance_granted       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
+   last_event_at           TIMESTAMPTZ,
+   last_global_position    BIGINT
+ );
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 6. Command Models (Pydantic)
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class SubmitApplicationCommand(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     applicant_id: str
+     requested_amount_usd: Decimal
+     loan_purpose: str
+     submission_channel: str
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+ 
+ class CreditAnalysisCompletedCommand(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     agent_id: str
+     session_id: str
+     model_version: str
+     confidence_score: float
+     risk_tier: str
+     recommended_limit_usd: Decimal
+     duration_ms: int
+     input_data: dict                  # raw; hashed inside handler
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+     causation_id: str | None = None
+ 
+ class FraudScreeningCompletedCommand(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     agent_id: str
+     session_id: str
+     fraud_score: float                # validated 0.0–1.0
+     anomaly_flags: list[str]
+     screening_model_version: str
+     input_data: dict
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+     causation_id: str | None = None
+ 
+ class GenerateDecisionCommand(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     orchestrator_agent_id: str
+     recommendation: str
+     confidence_score: float
+     contributing_agent_sessions: list[str]
+     decision_basis_summary: str
+     model_versions: dict[str, str]
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+     causation_id: str | None = None
+ 
+ class RecordHumanReviewCommand(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     reviewer_id: str                  # caller-supplied; stored verbatim
+     override: bool
+     final_decision: str
+     override_reason: str | None = None
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+ 
+ class StartAgentSessionCommand(BaseModel):
+     agent_id: str
+     session_id: str
+     context_source: str
+     event_replay_from_position: int = 0
+     context_token_count: int
+     model_version: str
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+ 
+ class RecordComplianceCheckCommand(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     rule_id: str
+     rule_version: str
+     passed: bool
+     evidence_hash: str | None = None
+     failure_reason: str | None = None
+     remediation_required: bool = False
+     regulation_set_version: str | None = None
+     evaluation_timestamp: datetime | None = None
+     correlation_id: str | None = None
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 7. Output / Result Types
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class IntegrityCheckResult(BaseModel):
+     entity_type: str
+     entity_id: str
+     events_verified: int
+     chain_valid: bool
+     tamper_detected: bool
+     integrity_hash: str
+     check_timestamp: datetime
+ 
+ class AgentContext(BaseModel):
+     agent_id: str
+     session_id: str
+     context_text: str                 # prose summary within token_budget
+     last_event_position: int
+     pending_work: list[str]
+     session_health_status: SessionHealthStatus
+ 
+ class WhatIfResult(BaseModel):
+     application_id: str
+     branch_event_type: str
+     real_outcome: dict                # ApplicationSummary-like state
+     counterfactual_outcome: dict
+     divergence_events: list[str]     # event_types that differed
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 8. Entity Relationship Summary
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ event_streams (1) ──── (N) events
+ events (1) ──── (0-1) outbox
+ projection_checkpoints (1 per projection name)
+ compliance_snapshots (N per application_id)
+ projection_errors (N per projection + event)
+ 
+ application_summary (1 per application_id) ← built from LoanApplication + AgentSession + ComplianceRecord events
+ agent_performance_ledger (1 per agent_id+model_version) ← built from AgentSession events
+ compliance_audit_view (1 per application_id) ← built from ComplianceRecord events
+ compliance_snapshots (N per application_id) ← point-in-time snapshots of compliance_audit_view state
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

@@ -1,1 +1,127 @@
- 
+ # Contract: EventStore Interface
+ 
+ **Status**: FROZEN after Phase 1 completion (Constitution Principle III)
+ **Source**: Challenge document Phase 1, "Core Python Interface"
+ **File**: &#96;src/event_store.py&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Class Signature
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
+ from src.models.events import BaseEvent, StoredEvent, StreamMetadata
+ from src.models.exceptions import OptimisticConcurrencyError, StreamNotFoundError
+ 
+ class EventStore:
+     def __init__(self, pool: asyncpg.Pool, upcaster_registry: "UpcasterRegistry") -&gt; None: ...
+ 
+     async def append(
+         self,
+         stream_id: str,
+         events: list[BaseEvent],
+         expected_version: int,          # -1 = new stream; N = exact version required
+         correlation_id: str | None = None,
+         causation_id:   str | None = None,
+     ) -&gt; int:
+         """
+         Atomically appends events to stream_id.
+         Raises OptimisticConcurrencyError if stream version != expected_version.
+         Writes to outbox in same transaction.
+         Returns new stream version.
+         """
+ 
+     async def load_stream(
+         self,
+         stream_id: str,
+         from_position: int = 0,
+         to_position:   int | None = None,
+     ) -&gt; list[StoredEvent]:
+         """
+         Returns events in stream order.
+         Each event is automatically upcasted via UpcasterRegistry before return.
+         Raises StreamNotFoundError if stream_id does not exist.
+         """
+ 
+     async def load_all(
+         self,
+         from_global_position: int = 0,
+         event_types: list[str] | None = None,
+         batch_size: int = 500,
+     ) -&gt; AsyncIterator[StoredEvent]:
+         """
+         Async generator; yields StoredEvents in global_position order.
+         Each event is automatically upcasted before yield.
+         Efficient for full replay — does not load all events into memory.
+         """
+ 
+     async def stream_version(self, stream_id: str) -&gt; int:
+         """Returns current_version from event_streams. Raises StreamNotFoundError."""
+ 
+     async def archive_stream(self, stream_id: str) -&gt; None:
+         """Sets archived_at on the stream. Archived streams cannot be appended to."""
+ 
+     async def get_stream_metadata(self, stream_id: str) -&gt; StreamMetadata:
+         """Returns StreamMetadata for stream_id. Raises StreamNotFoundError."""
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Behaviour Contracts
+ 
+ ### &#96;append&#96; — Optimistic Concurrency
+ 
+ | Condition | Behaviour |
+ |-----------|-----------|
+ | &#96;expected_version == -1&#96; AND stream does not exist | Create stream; assign &#96;stream_position&#96; starting at 1 |
+ | &#96;expected_version == -1&#96; AND stream exists | Raise &#96;OptimisticConcurrencyError(expected=-1, actual=current_version)&#96; |
+ | &#96;expected_version == N&#96; AND &#96;current_version == N&#96; | Append events starting at &#96;stream_position = N+1&#96;; update &#96;current_version&#96; |
+ | &#96;expected_version == N&#96; AND &#96;current_version != N&#96; | Raise &#96;OptimisticConcurrencyError(expected=N, actual=current_version)&#96; |
+ | Stream is archived | Raise &#96;DomainError(rule="archived_stream", message="Cannot append to archived stream")&#96; |
+ 
+ **Atomicity guarantee**: &#96;events&#96; rows + &#96;outbox&#96; rows are inserted in the
+ same &#96;asyncpg&#96; transaction. Either both commit or both roll back.
+ 
+ ### &#96;load_stream&#96; — Upcasting Transparency
+ 
+ Callers NEVER call upcasters manually. &#96;load_stream&#96; applies the upcaster
+ chain automatically. The raw stored payload is never returned to callers —
+ they always receive the latest-version representation.
+ 
+ ### &#96;load_all&#96; — Generator Protocol
+ 
+ Callers must &#96;async for event in store.load_all(...)&#96;. The generator fetches
+ events in &#96;batch_size&#96; pages from PostgreSQL, reducing memory footprint during
+ full replay. &#96;event_types&#96; filtering uses &#96;WHERE event_type = ANY($1)&#96; on the
+ indexed &#96;event_type&#96; column.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Error Types
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class OptimisticConcurrencyError(Exception):
+     stream_id: str
+     expected_version: int
+     actual_version: int
+     suggested_action: str = "reload_stream_and_retry"
+ 
+ class StreamNotFoundError(Exception):
+     stream_id: str
+ 
+ class DomainError(Exception):
+     rule: str       # machine-readable rule name
+     message: str    # human-readable description
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## SLO Commitments
+ 
+ | Operation | p99 Target | Notes |
+ |-----------|-----------|-------|
+ | &#96;append&#96; (single event, non-concurrent) | &lt; 10 ms | Local Postgres |
+ | &#96;load_stream&#96; (&lt; 100 events) | &lt; 50 ms | With upcasting |
+ | &#96;load_all&#96; (first batch yield) | &lt; 100 ms | Projection daemon replay |
+ | &#96;stream_version&#96; | &lt; 5 ms | Single row lookup |
+ 
```

</details>

- 
+ # Contract: Aggregate Interfaces
+ 
+ **Status**: FROZEN after Phase 2 completion (Constitution Principle III)
+ **Source**: Challenge document Phase 2
+ **Files**: &#96;src/aggregates/*.py&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Pattern Contract (All Aggregates)
+ 
+ Every aggregate MUST follow the &#96;load → validate → determine → append&#96; pattern.
+ No I/O is permitted inside &#96;_apply&#96; handlers or business rule assertion methods.
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ @classmethod
+ async def load(cls, store: EventStore, *ids: str) -&gt; "TAgg":
+     """Replay event stream to reconstruct current state."""
+ 
+ def _apply(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None:
+     """Dispatch to per-event-type handler; update version."""
+     handler = getattr(self, f"_on_{event.event_type}", None)
+     if handler:
+         handler(event)
+     self.version = event.stream_position
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## &#96;LoanApplicationAggregate&#96; Contract
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class LoanApplicationAggregate:
+     # State
+     application_id: str
+     state: ApplicationState
+     version: int
+     applicant_id: str | None
+     requested_amount: Decimal | None
+     approved_amount: Decimal | None
+     risk_tier: str | None
+     confidence_score: float | None
+     compliance_checks_required: list[str]
+     compliance_checks_passed: list[str]
+     credit_analysis_locked: bool
+     contributing_sessions: list[str]
+ 
+     # Construction
+     @classmethod
+     async def load(cls, store: EventStore, application_id: str) -&gt; "LoanApplicationAggregate": ...
+ 
+     # Business rule assertions (raise DomainError on violation)
+     def assert_state(self, expected: ApplicationState) -&gt; None: ...
+     def assert_awaiting_credit_analysis(self) -&gt; None: ...
+     def assert_analysis_not_locked(self) -&gt; None: ...
+     def assert_compliance_cleared(self) -&gt; None: ...
+     def assert_contributing_sessions_valid(self, session_ids: list[str]) -&gt; None: ...
+     def assert_can_approve(self) -&gt; None: ...
+ 
+     # Apply handlers (one per event type)
+     def _on_ApplicationSubmitted(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_CreditAnalysisRequested(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_AnalysisCompleted(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_DecisionGenerated(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_HumanReviewCompleted(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_ApplicationApproved(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_ApplicationDeclined(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_HumanReviewOverride(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Business Rules (enforced ONLY in aggregate assertions)
+ 
+ | Rule | Assertion Method | DomainError.rule |
+ |------|-----------------|------------------|
+ | BR-001 — State machine transitions | &#96;assert_state&#96; + &#96;_apply&#96; | &#96;"invalid_state_transition"&#96; |
+ | BR-002 — Credit analysis lock | &#96;assert_analysis_not_locked&#96; | &#96;"credit_analysis_locked"&#96; |
+ | BR-003 — Compliance dependency for approval | &#96;assert_compliance_cleared&#96; | &#96;"compliance_not_cleared"&#96; |
+ | BR-004 — Confidence floor | Checked in &#96;_on_DecisionGenerated&#96; before state update | &#96;"confidence_floor_violated"&#96; |
+ | BR-005 — Causal chain for contributing sessions | &#96;assert_contributing_sessions_valid&#96; | &#96;"invalid_causal_chain"&#96; |
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## &#96;AgentSessionAggregate&#96; Contract
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class AgentSessionAggregate:
+     # State
+     agent_id: str
+     session_id: str
+     version: int
+     context_loaded: bool
+     model_version: str | None
+     completed_application_ids: set[str]
+     health_status: SessionHealthStatus
+ 
+     # Construction
+     @classmethod
+     async def load(
+         cls,
+         store: EventStore,
+         agent_id: str,
+         session_id: str,
+     ) -&gt; "AgentSessionAggregate": ...
+ 
+     # Business rule assertions
+     def assert_context_loaded(self) -&gt; None: ...          # BR-006
+     def assert_model_version_current(self, model_version: str) -&gt; None: ...  # BR-002 complement
+ 
+     # Apply handlers
+     def _on_AgentContextLoaded(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_CreditAnalysisCompleted(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_FraudScreeningCompleted(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_DecisionGenerated(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Business Rules
+ 
+ | Rule | Assertion Method | DomainError.rule |
+ |------|-----------------|------------------|
+ | BR-006 — Agent context requirement (Gas Town) | &#96;assert_context_loaded&#96; | &#96;"context_not_loaded"&#96; |
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## &#96;ComplianceRecordAggregate&#96; Contract
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class ComplianceRecordAggregate:
+     application_id: str
+     version: int
+     regulation_set_version: str | None
+     checks_required: list[str]
+     checks_passed: list[str]
+     checks_failed: list[str]
+     clearance_granted: bool
+ 
+     @classmethod
+     async def load(
+         cls,
+         store: EventStore,
+         application_id: str,
+     ) -&gt; "ComplianceRecordAggregate": ...
+ 
+     def assert_all_checks_passed(self) -&gt; None: ...
+     def assert_rule_id_valid(self, rule_id: str) -&gt; None: ...
+ 
+     def _on_ComplianceCheckRequested(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_ComplianceRulePassed(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_ComplianceRuleFailed(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+     def _on_ComplianceClearanceGranted(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+ 
+     def is_cleared(self) -&gt; bool:
+         """True iff all checks_required are in checks_passed."""
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## &#96;AuditLedgerAggregate&#96; Contract
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ class AuditLedgerAggregate:
+     entity_type: str
+     entity_id: str
+     version: int
+     last_integrity_hash: str      # "0"*64 for genesis
+     last_check_at: datetime | None
+ 
+     @classmethod
+     async def load(
+         cls,
+         store: EventStore,
+         entity_type: str,
+         entity_id: str,
+     ) -&gt; "AuditLedgerAggregate": ...
+ 
+     def assert_rate_limit_not_exceeded(self) -&gt; None:
+         """Raises DomainError('rate_limited') if last_check_at was &lt; 60s ago."""
+ 
+     def _on_AuditIntegrityCheckRun(self, event: StoredEvent) -&gt; None: ...
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## What MUST NOT Be in Aggregates
+ 
+ - No &#96;EventStore&#96; I/O inside &#96;_apply&#96; or assertion methods
+ - No HTTP calls, no subprocess calls
+ - No Pydantic model construction (work with &#96;event.payload&#96; dict)
+ - No logging (aggregates are pure domain objects)
+ - No projection reads (aggregates load only their own stream)
+ 
```

</details>

@@ -1,1 +1,339 @@
- 
+ # Contract: MCP Tools (Command Side)
+ 
+ **Status**: FROZEN after Phase 5 completion (Constitution Principle III)
+ **Source**: Challenge document Phase 5, "MCP Tools — The Command Side"
+ **File**: &#96;src/mcp/tools.py&#96;
+ **Transport**: MCP (stdio or SSE)
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Design Principles
+ 
+ 1. **Zero business logic in tools**: All validation delegated to aggregates and
+    command handlers. Tools are thin orchestrators.
+ 2. **Structured error types**: Every error is a typed JSON object, not a string.
+    LLM consumers need machine-readable errors to reason about recovery.
+ 3. **Precondition documentation**: Each tool description explicitly states what
+    must be true before calling. LLM consumers read this as the contract.
+ 4. **Security model**: Caller-supplied identity (reviewer_id, agent_id). The MCP
+    server records identity in event payloads for audit but does not validate
+    credentials. Access control is a perimeter concern.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Structured Error Object (all tools)
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "error_type": "OptimisticConcurrencyError",
+   "message": "Stream version mismatch on loan-app-123",
+   "stream_id": "loan-app-123",
+   "expected_version": 3,
+   "actual_version": 5,
+   "suggested_action": "reload_stream_and_retry"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ All &#96;error_type&#96; values:
+ - &#96;OptimisticConcurrencyError&#96; → retry after reload
+ - &#96;DomainError&#96; → do not retry; fix the command
+ - &#96;PreconditionFailed&#96; → satisfy precondition first
+ - &#96;ValidationError&#96; → fix input data
+ - &#96;StreamNotFoundError&#96; → check stream_id
+ - &#96;RateLimitedError&#96; → wait and retry
+ - &#96;ConcurrencyExhaustedError&#96; → escalate to human
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 1: &#96;submit_application&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;ApplicationSubmitted&#96;
+ **Precondition**: None (first call in lifecycle)
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string (UUID or business key)",
+   "applicant_id": "string",
+   "requested_amount_usd": "string (decimal)",
+   "loan_purpose": "string",
+   "submission_channel": "string ('web'|'api'|'branch')",
+   "correlation_id": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Validation**: Pydantic &#96;SubmitApplicationCommand&#96;; duplicate &#96;application_id&#96;
+ check (expected_version=-1 on append enforces uniqueness).
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "stream_id": "loan-app-123",
+   "initial_version": 1
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Tool description for LLM**:
+ &gt; Submits a new commercial loan application to The Ledger. Creates a new
+ &gt; LoanApplication stream. If application_id already exists, returns
+ &gt; OptimisticConcurrencyError. This is always the first tool called in the
+ &gt; loan lifecycle.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 2: &#96;start_agent_session&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;AgentContextLoaded&#96;
+ **Precondition**: None (but MUST be called before any agent decision tools)
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "agent_id": "string",
+   "session_id": "string (UUID recommended)",
+   "context_source": "string ('event_replay'|'external_knowledge'|'hybrid')",
+   "event_replay_from_position": "integer (default 0)",
+   "context_token_count": "integer",
+   "model_version": "string",
+   "correlation_id": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "session_id": "string",
+   "context_position": "integer"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Tool description for LLM**:
+ &gt; Starts an agent work session and loads context into the AgentSession stream.
+ &gt; MUST be called before record_credit_analysis, record_fraud_screening, or
+ &gt; generate_decision. The session persists in the event store even across process
+ &gt; restarts (Gas Town pattern). If an existing session exists for agent_id +
+ &gt; session_id, returns the existing session's context_position.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 3: &#96;record_credit_analysis&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;CreditAnalysisCompleted&#96;
+ **Precondition**: Active AgentSession for agent_id+session_id with context loaded.
+ loan stream must exist (submit_application called first).
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "agent_id": "string",
+   "session_id": "string",
+   "model_version": "string",
+   "confidence_score": "float (0.0–1.0)",
+   "risk_tier": "string ('LOW'|'MEDIUM'|'HIGH'|'VERY_HIGH')",
+   "recommended_limit_usd": "string (decimal)",
+   "duration_ms": "integer",
+   "input_data": "object (will be hashed)",
+   "correlation_id": "string | null",
+   "causation_id": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Validation**: Agent session active + context loaded; credit analysis not already
+ locked for this application; optimistic concurrency on loan stream.
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "event_id": "UUID",
+   "new_stream_version": "integer"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Tool description for LLM**:
+ &gt; Records a completed credit analysis for a loan application. Requires an active
+ &gt; agent session created by start_agent_session — calling without an active session
+ &gt; returns PreconditionFailed. If credit analysis has already been recorded for
+ &gt; this application (and not overridden), returns DomainError with rule
+ &gt; 'credit_analysis_locked'. Handles OptimisticConcurrencyError automatically
+ &gt; with up to 3 retries.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 4: &#96;record_fraud_screening&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;FraudScreeningCompleted&#96;
+ **Precondition**: Active AgentSession for agent_id+session_id with context loaded.
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "agent_id": "string",
+   "session_id": "string",
+   "fraud_score": "float (0.0–1.0 validated)",
+   "anomaly_flags": ["string"],
+   "screening_model_version": "string",
+   "input_data": "object",
+   "correlation_id": "string | null",
+   "causation_id": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Validation**: &#96;fraud_score&#96; must be in &#96;[0.0, 1.0]&#96;; agent session active +
+ context loaded.
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "event_id": "UUID",
+   "new_stream_version": "integer"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 5: &#96;record_compliance_check&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;ComplianceRulePassed&#96; or &#96;ComplianceRuleFailed&#96;
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "rule_id": "string",
+   "rule_version": "string",
+   "passed": "boolean",
+   "evidence_hash": "string | null",
+   "failure_reason": "string | null",
+   "remediation_required": "boolean (default false)",
+   "regulation_set_version": "string | null",
+   "evaluation_timestamp": "string (ISO datetime) | null",
+   "correlation_id": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Validation**: &#96;rule_id&#96; must be in the application's &#96;checks_required&#96; list
+ (loaded from ComplianceRecord stream).
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "check_id": "UUID",
+   "compliance_status": "PENDING | PASSED | FAILED"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 6: &#96;generate_decision&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;DecisionGenerated&#96;
+ **Precondition**: Active AgentSession. All required agent analyses must be present
+ on the loan stream. confidence_floor &lt; 0.6 forces REFER regardless of input.
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "orchestrator_agent_id": "string",
+   "recommendation": "string ('APPROVE'|'DECLINE'|'REFER')",
+   "confidence_score": "float (0.0–1.0)",
+   "contributing_agent_sessions": ["string (stream IDs)"],
+   "decision_basis_summary": "string",
+   "model_versions": {"agent_role": "model_version_string"},
+   "correlation_id": "string | null",
+   "causation_id": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "decision_id": "UUID",
+   "recommendation": "APPROVE | DECLINE | REFER"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Tool description for LLM**:
+ &gt; Generates the final AI recommendation for a loan application. Requires all
+ &gt; agent analyses (credit, fraud) to be recorded first — calling before analyses
+ &gt; are present returns PreconditionFailed. IMPORTANT: if confidence_score &lt; 0.6,
+ &gt; the recommendation is automatically overridden to 'REFER' regardless of input
+ &gt; (regulatory requirement). contributing_agent_sessions must reference session
+ &gt; stream IDs that actually processed this application_id — invalid references
+ &gt; return DomainError with rule 'invalid_causal_chain'.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 7: &#96;record_human_review&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;HumanReviewCompleted&#96;
+ **Precondition**: Application must be in ApprovedPendingHuman or
+ DeclinedPendingHuman state.
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "reviewer_id": "string (caller-supplied; stored verbatim)",
+   "override": "boolean",
+   "final_decision": "string ('APPROVE'|'DECLINE')",
+   "override_reason": "string | null (required if override=true)"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Validation**: If &#96;override=true&#96;, &#96;override_reason&#96; must be non-empty (enforced
+ by aggregate). Application state must allow human review.
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "final_decision": "APPROVE | DECLINE",
+   "application_state": "FinalApproved | FinalDeclined"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tool 8: &#96;run_integrity_check&#96;
+ 
+ **Command**: &#96;AuditIntegrityCheckRun&#96;
+ **Precondition**: Rate-limited to 1 call per minute per entity (enforced by
+ AuditLedgerAggregate assertion).
+ 
+ **Input schema**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "entity_type": "string",
+   "entity_id": "string",
+   "role": "string (caller-supplied; should be 'compliance')"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Validation**: Rate limit enforced; &#96;role&#96; is stored in metadata verbatim
+ (no server-side role validation — perimeter concern).
+ 
+ **Return**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "check_result": {
+     "entity_type": "string",
+     "entity_id": "string",
+     "events_verified": "integer",
+     "chain_valid": "boolean",
+     "tamper_detected": "boolean",
+     "integrity_hash": "string",
+     "check_timestamp": "ISO datetime"
+   },
+   "chain_valid": "boolean"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Tool description for LLM**:
+ &gt; Runs a cryptographic audit chain integrity check on all events for the specified
+ &gt; entity. Rate-limited to 1 call per minute per entity — calling more frequently
+ &gt; returns RateLimitedError. Records the result as an AuditIntegrityCheckRun event
+ &gt; in the AuditLedger stream. If tamper_detected=true, the chain is broken and a
+ &gt; compliance incident should be escalated.
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

- 
+ # Contract: MCP Resources (Query Side)
+ 
+ **Status**: FROZEN after Phase 5 completion (Constitution Principle III)
+ **Source**: Challenge document Phase 5, "MCP Resources — The Query Side"
+ **File**: &#96;src/mcp/resources.py&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Design Principles
+ 
+ 1. **Projections only, no stream reads**: Resources MUST read from projection
+    tables (ApplicationSummary, AgentPerformanceLedger, ComplianceAuditView).
+    No &#96;store.load_stream()&#96; calls except the two documented justified exceptions.
+ 2. **SLO-bound**: Each resource has a p99 latency target; the &#96;ledger/health&#96;
+    resource must be &lt; 10 ms (watchdog).
+ 3. **Temporal queries**: Two resources support &#96;?as_of=&#96; and &#96;?from=&amp;to=&#96; query
+    parameters for regulatory time-travel.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Resource 1: &#96;ledger://applications/{id}&#96;
+ 
+ **Projection source**: &#96;application_summary&#96; table
+ **Temporal query**: No — current state only
+ **SLO**: p99 &lt; 50 ms
+ 
+ **Response**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "state": "string",
+   "applicant_id": "string",
+   "requested_amount_usd": "string (decimal)",
+   "approved_amount_usd": "string (decimal) | null",
+   "risk_tier": "string | null",
+   "fraud_score": "string (decimal) | null",
+   "compliance_status": "PENDING | PASSED | FAILED",
+   "decision": "APPROVE | DECLINE | REFER | null",
+   "agent_sessions_completed": ["stream_id"],
+   "last_event_type": "string",
+   "last_event_at": "ISO datetime",
+   "human_reviewer_id": "string | null",
+   "final_decision_at": "ISO datetime | null",
+   "_meta": {
+     "projection_lag_ms": "integer",
+     "as_of": "ISO datetime (current time)"
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Error**: &#96;404 StreamNotFoundError&#96; if &#96;application_id&#96; not in projection.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Resource 2: &#96;ledger://applications/{id}/compliance&#96;
+ 
+ **Projection source**: &#96;compliance_audit_view&#96; table + &#96;compliance_snapshots&#96;
+ **Temporal query**: Yes — &#96;?as_of=&lt;ISO datetime&gt;&#96;
+ **SLO**: p99 &lt; 200 ms
+ 
+ **Response** (current state):
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "regulation_set_version": "string",
+   "checks_required": ["rule_id"],
+   "checks_passed": [
+     {
+       "rule_id": "string",
+       "rule_version": "string",
+       "evaluation_timestamp": "ISO datetime",
+       "evidence_hash": "string"
+     }
+   ],
+   "checks_failed": [
+     {
+       "rule_id": "string",
+       "rule_version": "string",
+       "failure_reason": "string",
+       "remediation_required": "boolean"
+     }
+   ],
+   "clearance_granted": "boolean",
+   "last_event_at": "ISO datetime",
+   "_meta": {
+     "projection_lag_ms": "integer",
+     "as_of": "ISO datetime (now or ?as_of value)",
+     "snapshot_used": "boolean"
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Temporal query**: With &#96;?as_of=2026-01-15T10:00:00Z&#96;, returns compliance state
+ as it existed at that timestamp. Uses &#96;compliance_snapshots&#96; + event replay from
+ nearest preceding snapshot.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Resource 3: &#96;ledger://applications/{id}/audit-trail&#96;
+ 
+ **Projection source**: &#96;AuditLedger&#96; stream (direct load — justified exception)
+ **Temporal query**: Yes — &#96;?from=&lt;ISO&gt;&amp;to=&lt;ISO&gt;&#96; range filter
+ **SLO**: p99 &lt; 500 ms
+ **Justified exception**: AuditLedger direct load is explicitly documented in the
+ challenge spec as a justified exception. The audit trail's value IS the
+ chronological event stream; no projection adds value here.
+ 
+ **Response**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "application_id": "string",
+   "events": [
+     {
+       "event_id": "UUID",
+       "event_type": "string",
+       "event_version": "integer",
+       "stream_id": "string",
+       "stream_position": "integer",
+       "global_position": "integer",
+       "recorded_at": "ISO datetime",
+       "payload": {},
+       "metadata": {
+         "correlation_id": "string | null",
+         "causation_id": "string | null"
+       }
+     }
+   ],
+   "integrity_status": {
+     "last_check_at": "ISO datetime | null",
+     "chain_valid": "boolean | null",
+     "events_since_last_check": "integer"
+   },
+   "_meta": {
+     "total_events": "integer",
+     "from": "ISO datetime | null",
+     "to": "ISO datetime | null"
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Resource 4: &#96;ledger://agents/{id}/performance&#96;
+ 
+ **Projection source**: &#96;agent_performance_ledger&#96; table
+ **Temporal query**: No — current metrics only
+ **SLO**: p99 &lt; 50 ms
+ 
+ **Response**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "agent_id": "string",
+   "model_versions": [
+     {
+       "model_version": "string",
+       "analyses_completed": "integer",
+       "decisions_generated": "integer",
+       "avg_confidence_score": "float | null",
+       "avg_duration_ms": "float | null",
+       "approve_rate": "float | null",
+       "decline_rate": "float | null",
+       "refer_rate": "float | null",
+       "human_override_rate": "float | null",
+       "first_seen_at": "ISO datetime",
+       "last_seen_at": "ISO datetime"
+     }
+   ]
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Resource 5: &#96;ledger://agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}&#96;
+ 
+ **Projection source**: &#96;AgentSession&#96; stream (direct load — justified exception)
+ **Temporal query**: Yes — full replay capability
+ **SLO**: p99 &lt; 300 ms
+ **Justified exception**: Full agent session replay is the Gas Town recovery
+ mechanism. The value is the complete ordered event history, not a summarised view.
+ 
+ **Response**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "agent_id": "string",
+   "session_id": "string",
+   "events": [
+     {
+       "event_id": "UUID",
+       "event_type": "string",
+       "stream_position": "integer",
+       "recorded_at": "ISO datetime",
+       "payload": {}
+     }
+   ],
+   "health_status": "ACTIVE | NEEDS_RECONCILIATION | COMPLETED",
+   "context_loaded": "boolean",
+   "model_version": "string | null"
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Resource 6: &#96;ledger://ledger/health&#96;
+ 
+ **Projection source**: &#96;ProjectionDaemon.get_all_lags()&#96;
+ **Temporal query**: No — real-time only
+ **SLO**: p99 &lt; 10 ms (watchdog endpoint — must be fastest in the system)
+ **No database queries**: Returns cached in-memory lag values from the daemon.
+ 
+ **Response**:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "status": "healthy | degraded | critical",
+   "projections": {
+     "application_summary": {
+       "lag_ms": "integer",
+       "slo_ms": 500,
+       "slo_breached": "boolean",
+       "last_position": "integer",
+       "last_updated_at": "ISO datetime"
+     },
+     "agent_performance_ledger": {
+       "lag_ms": "integer",
+       "slo_ms": 500,
+       "slo_breached": "boolean",
+       "last_position": "integer",
+       "last_updated_at": "ISO datetime"
+     },
+     "compliance_audit_view": {
+       "lag_ms": "integer",
+       "slo_ms": 2000,
+       "slo_breached": "boolean",
+       "last_position": "integer",
+       "last_updated_at": "ISO datetime"
+     }
+   },
+   "event_store": {
+     "latest_global_position": "integer",
+     "total_streams": "integer"
+   },
+   "_meta": {
+     "checked_at": "ISO datetime"
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Status logic**:
+ - &#96;healthy&#96;: no SLO breaches
+ - &#96;degraded&#96;: one or more projections exceed SLO by &lt; 3×
+ - &#96;critical&#96;: one or more projections exceed SLO by ≥ 3× for ≥ 5 consecutive cycles
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>
